differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (pyoderma gangrenosum, acne, and hidradenitis suppurativa)
next_steps: Punch biopsy of the ulcer edge for histopathology and tissue culture
Skin and tissue biopsy for tissue immunohistochemistry and special stains
Evaluation for underlying inflammatory bowel disease (colonoscopy/fecal calprotectin)
Discontinuation of unnecessary antibiotics and initiation of anti-inflammatory treatment
Wound care management to prevent secondary infection

differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Hidradenitis suppurativa)
Crohn's disease with systemic and cutaneous manifestations
SAPHO syndrome (Synovitis, Acne, Pustulosis, Hyperostosis, and Osteitis)
next_steps: Punch biopsy of the active edge of the skin ulcers for histopathology, tissue culture (bacterial, fungal, mycobacterial), and immunofluorescence
Initiate systemic glucocorticoid therapy (e.g., high-dose intravenous methylprednisolone) following exclusion of deep infection
Perform colonoscopy and fecal calprotectin testing to investigate for occult inflammatory bowel disease
Consultation with dermatology, rheumatology, and gastroenterology
Monitor serum inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) to assess disease activity and treatment response
Discontinue broad-spectrum antibiotics unless active infection is confirmed
Genetic testing for autoinflammatory syndrome mutations (e.g., PSTPIP1 gene for PAPA/PASH) if clinical suspicion remains high
